steps.title: Spot the cause-and-effect relationship
steps.content: The first sentence establishes Green's goal: avoid harsh synthetic dyes. The second describes her action: experimenting with natural alternatives. The transition must show that her action is a response to her goal.

steps.title: Select the purpose transition
steps.content:
The logical relationship is cause-effect/purpose — Green's goal (avoid harsh synthetic dyes) directly motivates her action (experimenting with natural dyes). The blank must show the action was taken to fulfill that goal.
  • Choice A — In any case,: signals that what follows is true regardless of prior context, dismissing the relevance of the goal rather than connecting to it. ✗
  • Choice B — To that end,: signals that the action was taken specifically to achieve the previously stated goal — a precise purpose link between avoiding synthetic dyes and experimenting with natural ones. ✓
  • Choice C — On the other hand,: signals contrast between two equally valid positions; Green's experimenting is not a contrasting viewpoint but a direct response to her goal. ✗
  • Choice D — In reality,: signals a correction to a misconception, implying the first sentence was mistaken or misleading, which it is not. ✗
That is the correct answer.
